nameMeanings.name: Rokeley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Coakley  .

variants.name: Coakley
meanings: Irish (Cork): Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Caochlaoich, a patronymic from Caochlaoch, a personal name composed of the elements caoch ‘blind’ + laoch ‘warrior, hero’.
Irish: variant of Cloughley, possibly a shortened form of an unidentified Irish surname in Mac-.

nameMeanings.name: Anne
meanings: A name corrpted from Hannah, q.v. It is also found several times in a male name. From Ann, Anne, we have as nicknames Nan and Nancy

variants.name: Nancy
meanings: Of uncertain origin. From the 18th century it is clearly used as a pet form of   Ann   (  see  Nan  ), but it may originally have been a similar formation deriving from the common medieval given name   Annis  , a vernacular form of   Agnes .  Nowadays it is an independent name, and was especially popular in America in the 1930s, 40s, and 50s.

variants.name: Nan
meanings: Originally a pet form of Ann (for the initial N-, compare Ned ). It is now also widely used as a short form of Nancy .

en.lifeSummary: Anne Rokeley was born about 1268, in Frampton by Cirencester, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom as the daughter of Thomas Rokeley and Mrs Thomas Rokeley. She had at least 1 daughter with John Huntingfield.
